page: Amazon Web Services r8gb.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and custom AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors. Operating on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 96 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The processor configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance alongside average single-core capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier status in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, PassMark memory scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with both small-block cached and large-block uncached read/write operations placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Database operations, including Redis SET operations and PassMark database tests, also rank in the top tier. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench text processing scoring in the average range. For large language model inference, text generation using medium and large models achieves top-tier speeds, whereas text generation with small models falls into the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8gb.24xlarge offers high resource density by combining 96 dedicated physical ARM cores with a substantial 768.0 GB memory footprint, making it highly efficient for memory-bound workloads that scale horizontally across many cores. The absence of local storage and GPU hardware means users do not pay for unutilized accelerators or local disks, relying instead on network-attached storage. This hardware profile is suited for large-scale database deployments, in-memory caching systems like Redis, multi-threaded software compilation, and medium-to-large scale LLM text generation workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 dedicated physical ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 cores running at 2.8 GHz on the arm64 architecture. Managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 768.0 GB of memory, providing 8.0 GB per core, with a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps and no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and top-tier database operations, though single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation are relatively weaker. This resource configuration delivers high memory density and multi-threaded efficiency, making it suitable for in-memory databases, Redis caching, software compilation, and medium-to-large LLM text generation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.24xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance with 96 dedicated Graviton cores, 768 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.

tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized platform featuring ninety-six dedicated physical cores, high-bandwidth memory, and high-speed networking for demanding database workloads.
